summary: Tested Google OAuth integration and traditional auth for RoutineQuest. All 11 backend tests passed. All 4 frontend tests passed. Google Sign-In button visible, redirects correctly to auth.emergentagent.com. Traditional login works for both rafael (admin) and gabriela (gabriela).

backend.test_google_auth_endpoint_exists: PASS - /api/auth/google accepts POST, returns 400 for invalid session
backend.test_get_data_with_auth: PASS - returns data with valid token

frontend.google_signin_button_visible: PASS - Button with data-testid='google-signin-btn' visible, text='Continue with Google'
frontend.rafael_traditional_login: PASS - Login with rafael/admin shows dashboard with 'Good evening, Rafael!'
frontend.gabriela_traditional_login: PASS - Login with gabriela/gabriela shows pink-themed dashboard

critical_code_review_comments: IMPORTANT: Seed data in server.py line 228 shows rafael's password is 'admin', NOT 'rafael' as stated in the request. The request credentials are incorrect.
Google OAuth uses MOCKED Emergent Auth service (auth.emergentagent.com) - not real Google OAuth
context_for_next_testing_agent: Google OAuth integration complete and working. Button redirects to auth.emergentagent.com. Backend /api/auth/google endpoint accepts session_id and exchanges it with Emergent Auth service. Traditional login works for both users. Note: rafael's password is 'admin' not 'rafael'.

mocked_apis.Google OAuth: Uses Emergent Auth (auth.emergentagent.com) which provides a MOCKED but realistic OAuth flow
